pattern: Build a 2-D prefix sum so any square's sum can be fetched in O(1), then binary-search the side length from 1 to min(m, n) — feasibility is monotone so binary search is valid.
hints: Any submatrix sum is efficiently retrieved with a prefix-sum table: psum[r][c] = sum of the rectangle from (0,0) to (r-1,c-1); the square from (r,c) to (r+k-1,c+k-1) is psum[r+k][c+k] - psum[r][c+k] - psum[r+k][c] + psum[r][c].
If a side of length k fits under the threshold somewhere, then any side k' < k also fits — so binary-search for the largest valid k.
Build the prefix sum table, then binary-search on side length: for each candidate length check every valid top-left corner for a qualifying square.

edge_inputs.description: 1x1 matrix, single cell at or below threshold
threshold zero, only zero-value cells qualify
answer is 0 because every single cell exceeds threshold
non-square matrix; side cannot exceed min(m,n)

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, size)
    n = rng.randint(1, size)
    mat = [[rng.randint(0, 10000) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    threshold = rng.randint(0, 100000)
    return [mat, threshold]
